div.head: The Minister in Switzerland (Harrison) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 7871. American interests Japan and occupied territory, inspection of prisoner of war camps. Foreign Office official handed TaitGeorge Tait, First Secretary of Legation in Switzerland. December 14 notice to the effect that Gorgé reports full accounts Japanese prisoner camps never visited and certain others for example those Tokyo and Yokohama have not been inspected for more than a year. This situation greatly concerns Gorgé who does not miss single opportunity personally insist with Japanese Foreign Office obtain necessary authorization visit. He fears prisoners of war have impression he is not doing all possible being [bring?] moral comfort which they doubtlessly need greatly.
Under these circumstances he suggests British and American Governments again insist authorization visit prisoners of war camps be given Swiss representatives.
British Legation received similar communication December 10 and telegraphed London same day.
